visa_types.name: Tourism
visa_types.documents_required:
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate documents.
Additionally for Diplomatic and Special Passport holders- 
																	Verbal Note from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Bahrain is required to be submitted.
General requirements for all applicants of tourist  visa applications
Click here for Tourist Visa checklist
You are eligible to apply for a visa at the Swiss Embassy if either the majority of your total stay is in Switzerland or if it equals the number of nights with a following
                                                                        Schengen country and it is the first destination on the tour.
In accordance with the agreement of 6th November, 1963 between Switzerland and the Principality of Liechtenstein, Swiss entry regulations also apply to applications to enter the Principality
                                                                        of Liechtenstein. 
Please note that additional documents might be requested.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Please Note: There is a service charge of BHD 9.300 per application (inclusive of 10% VAT) over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants.

[Table:  Tourism]
The Visa fees are to be paid in Bahraini Dinar by cash or card at the visa application centre. Service charges can be paid optionally online while booking an appointment. Charges are subject to change without notice
Terms and conditions
The service charges are non-refundable in the following situations and a new appointment will have to be scheduled:
- If you miss your appointment on the scheduled date
- If you are delayed for your appointment and cannot be accommodated on the same day
- If your application is incomplete as per the guidelines and the application cannot be accepted
- If you decide not to proceed with the application
- If you cancel your appointment
Applicants may also avail of additional services which attract associated fees. See: Additional Services Check Here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/che/buy-services)
visa_types.forms: Click Here to download Visa Application Form
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Manama.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
 All other applicants require an appointment to submit their application. Click Here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/che/login) to book your appointment.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Manama.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
 All other applicants require an appointment to submit their application. Click Here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/che/login) to book your appointment.

## Visa Fees
Please Note: There is a service charge of BHD 9.300 per application (inclusive of 10% VAT) over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants.

[Table:  Tourism]
The Visa fees are to be paid in Bahraini Dinar by cash or card at the visa application centre. Service charges can be paid optionally online while booking an appointment. Charges are subject to change without notice
Terms and conditions
The service charges are non-refundable in the following situations and a new appointment will have to be scheduled:
- If you miss your appointment on the scheduled date
- If you are delayed for your appointment and cannot be accommodated on the same day
- If your application is incomplete as per the guidelines and the application cannot be accepted
- If you decide not to proceed with the application
- If you cancel your appointment
Applicants may also avail of additional services which attract associated fees. See: Additional Services Check Here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/bhr/en/che/buy-services)

## Documents Required
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate documents.
Additionally for Diplomatic and Special Passport holders- 
																	Verbal Note from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Bahrain is required to be submitted.
General requirements for all applicants of tourist  visa applications
Click here for Tourist Visa checklist
You are eligible to apply for a visa at the Swiss Embassy if either the majority of your total stay is in Switzerland or if it equals the number of nights with a following
                                                                        Schengen country and it is the first destination on the tour.
In accordance with the agreement of 6th November, 1963 between Switzerland and the Principality of Liechtenstein, Swiss entry regulations also apply to applications to enter the Principality
                                                                        of Liechtenstein. 
Please note that additional documents might be requested.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer to attached document to know about acceptable photograph specifications as required to apply for Swiss visa.
Click Here

## Processing Times
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and, if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
Click Here to download Visa Application Form

visa_types.name: Visa Types
visa_types.overview:
SCHENGEN VISAS:
Currently, the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi, is competent for examining and deciding on applications for visiting Switzerland as main destination.
Foreign nationals who wish to travel to Switzerland have to be in possession of a Schengen visa, unless they are nationals from countries which are not included in the list of countries with the
                                                            obligation to carry a visa in order to cross external borders for stays in the Schengen Area of no more than three months (90 days) in a period of six months (180 days) from the date of entry
                                                            into the Schengen Area. 
Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for the Schengen visa at the Embassy of Switzerland and through the Visa Application Center.
Due to the increasing volume of applications, the number of appointments for applying for a Schengen visa at the Embassy's office has been restricted in order to provide a good service. For that
                                                            reason, kindly anticipate well in advance and apply AT LEAST 15 labour days before expected date of travel in order to avoid any future disappointment. Applications may also be submitted at the
                                                            VFS Global (as an outsourced partner by the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi.) as per the instructions given in the web page.
The same can be also submitted at the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi only with prior appointment requested at least 1 month before the proposed date of travel (no applicants will be received
                                                            at the Embassy without advanced registration and no applications will be received after visa office hours!).
visa_types.full_text:
## SCHENGEN VISAS:
Currently, the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi, is competent for examining and deciding on applications for visiting Switzerland as main destination.
Foreign nationals who wish to travel to Switzerland have to be in possession of a Schengen visa, unless they are nationals from countries which are not included in the list of countries with the
                                                            obligation to carry a visa in order to cross external borders for stays in the Schengen Area of no more than three months (90 days) in a period of six months (180 days) from the date of entry
                                                            into the Schengen Area. 
Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for the Schengen visa at the Embassy of Switzerland and through the Visa Application Center.
Due to the increasing volume of applications, the number of appointments for applying for a Schengen visa at the Embassy's office has been restricted in order to provide a good service. For that
                                                            reason, kindly anticipate well in advance and apply AT LEAST 15 labour days before expected date of travel in order to avoid any future disappointment. Applications may also be submitted at the
                                                            VFS Global (as an outsourced partner by the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi.) as per the instructions given in the web page.
The same can be also submitted at the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi only with prior appointment requested at least 1 month before the proposed date of travel (no applicants will be received
                                                            at the Embassy without advanced registration and no applications will be received after visa office hours!).

## NATIONAL VISAS:
Application forms for National Visas have to be submitted at the Embassy of Switzerland, located in Abu Dhabi.

visa_types.name: Short Term Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
Currently, the Consular Regional Center GCC at the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi is competent for examining and deciding on applications for visiting Switzerland as main destination.

Foreign nationals who wish to travel to those countries (mentioned above) have to be in possession of a Schengen visa, unless they are nationals from countries which are not included in the list of countries with the obligation to carry a visa in order to cross external borders for stays in the Schengen Area of no more than three months (90 days) in a period of six months (180 days) from the date of entry into the Schengen Area. 

Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for the Schengen visa at the Embassy of Switzerland, Abu Dhabi and through the Visa Application Centre in Manama, Bahrain. 

Due to the increasing volume of applications, the number of appointments for applying for a Schengen visa at the Embassy's office has been restricted in order to provide a good service.
                                                            For that reason, kindly anticipate well in advance and apply AT LEAST 15 working days before expected date of travel in order to avoid any future disappointment. Applications may also be submitted
                                                            at the VFS Global (as an outsourced partner by the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi ) as per the instructions given in the web page. 

The same can be also submitted at the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi only with prior appointment requested at least 1 month before the proposed date of travel (no applicants will be
                                                            received at the Embassy without advanced registration and no applications will be received after visa office hours!).

NATIONAL VISAS:
Application forms for National Visas have to be submitted at the Embassy of Switzerland, located in Abu Dhabi.

Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for the Schengen visa at the Embassy of Switzerland, Abu Dhabi and through the Visa Application Centre in Manama, Bahrain. 

Due to the increasing volume of applications, the number of appointments for applying for a Schengen visa at the Embassy's office has been restricted in order to provide a good service.
                                                            For that reason, kindly anticipate well in advance and apply AT LEAST 15 working days before expected date of travel in order to avoid any future disappointment. Applications may also be submitted
                                                            at the VFS Global (as an outsourced partner by the Embassy of Switzerland in Abu Dhabi ) as per the instructions given in the web page.

Application forms for National Visas have to be submitted at the Embassy of Switzerland, located in Abu Dhabi.

## Processing Times
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
